---
title: "Karissa Huang"
image: "files/images/kh-pic.JPG"
page-navigation: false
toc: false
#sidebar: false
page-layout: full

about:
  id: hero-heading
  template: trestles
  image-width: 70%
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: E-mail
      href: mailto:krhuang@berkeley.edu
    - text: "{{< ai google-scholar >}} Google Scholar"
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=z9_mebkAAAAJ&hl=en
      target: _blank
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/karissa-huang/
      target: _blank
---

:::{#hero-heading}

I am a fifth year PhD student in the Statistics Department at UC Berkeley, advised by [Christian Borgs](http://christianborgs.com/) and [Jennifer Chayes](http://jenniferchayes.com/).

I am broadly interested in using probability theory and statistics to understand the behavior of complex systems in computer science, economics, and public health. My work spans both theory and applications, with recent interests including networks, social behavior, and epidemiological models.

Before coming to Berkeley I received my undergraduate degree from Harvard, where I was advised by [Subhabrata Sen](https://sites.harvard.edu/sus977/). I've also spent time as an Applied Science intern at LinkedIn.

My research is supported in part by the NSF GRFP.


Email: krhuang at berkeley dot edu
:::
